articles.title: Prediction Markets are a National Security Threat
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets could compromise national security by revealing sensitive information or manipulating public opinion on critical issues. If regulators buy this narrative, we could see new restrictions that kill liquidity and force the most interesting political and geopolitical markets offshore.

articles.title: Beast Industries fires MrBeast editor after Kalshi insider trading accusation
articles.summary: Beast Industries terminated a video editor following allegations that the employee engaged in insider trading on Kalshi prediction markets. The firing signals that even entertainment companies now view prediction market manipulation as serious enough to warrant immediate termination, likely pushing platforms toward stricter employee trading restrictions.

articles.title: Pa. lawmaker proposes regulating prediction markets in Pennsylvania
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania legislator wants to create regulatory framework for prediction markets in the state. This could open up another major US market for platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, potentially adding millions more users to the prediction economy.

articles.title: The Wildest Frat Party on Campus? Prediction Markets
articles.summary: College campuses are becoming unexpected hotbeds for prediction market activity, with students treating betting on everything from campus events to national politics like the ultimate social competition. This matters because young, digitally native users drive adoption of new platforms, and their gambling habits today will shape the prediction market landscape for the next decade.

articles.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran War in the AI Era, Prediction Market Ethics, and Paramount Beats Netflix
articles.summary: Iran tensions are heating up just as AI warfare capabilities reach unsettling new heights, while prediction markets grapple with thorny ethical questions about betting on human conflict. The combination creates a perfect storm for traders: geopolitical volatility meets technological disruption meets moral hazard, all while entertainment giants like Paramount somehow outperform Netflix in an increasingly unpredictable media landscape.

articles.title: A Technology for a Low-Trust Society
articles.summary: Prediction markets are emerging as the go-to solution for navigating a world where traditional institutions have lost credibility and information sources can't be trusted. When people stop believing experts and media, they'll bet real money on outcomes, creating more reliable signals than any poll or pundit ever could.

articles.title: Kalshi faces backlash over handling of $54M market on Khamenei’s ouster
articles.summary: Kalshi is getting hammered by users after controversial decisions around its $54M market betting on whether Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei gets ousted from power. The blowback highlights how platform operators can torch user trust when they fumble high-stakes geopolitical markets, especially ones where resolution criteria get murky fast.

articles.title: Michigan sues Kalshi, alleges app is skirting state's gambling laws
articles.summary: Michigan's attorney general is going after Kalshi, claiming the prediction market platform violates the state's gambling regulations. This could set a dangerous precedent for other states looking to crack down on prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to geofence entire states or face costly legal battles.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Let You Bet on Anything, but This AI Stock Is the Surest Bet I've Seen
articles.summary: A prediction market bettor claims they've found the most reliable AI stock play available for wagering. The call matters because AI stocks have been driving massive betting volume across platforms, and if someone's cracked the code on consistent winners, other bettors will pile in fast.

articles.title: Understanding prediction markets with Louis Pellegrino and Charles Farrell
articles.summary: Louis Pellegrino and Charles Farrell break down how prediction markets actually work, from basic mechanics to advanced trading strategies. Smart money follows the insights of experienced traders who've learned to spot inefficiencies and capitalize on crowd psychology in these markets.

articles.title: Polymarket Gets Major Prediction Completely Wrong After Loudly Boasting About It
articles.summary: Polymarket hyped up their forecasting accuracy on a high-profile event, then completely whiffed when the actual outcome rolled around. The embarrassing miss undermines the platform's credibility pitch just as mainstream adoption hangs on whether prediction markets can actually deliver better forecasts than traditional polling and punditry.

articles.title: Prediction markets are playing a dangerous game
articles.summary: Prediction markets are facing mounting regulatory pressure as lawmakers worry about their influence on elections and potential for manipulation. If regulators crack down hard, we could see major platforms shut down or forced overseas, leaving US bettors with fewer options and less liquidity.
